mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 52% accuracy over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both series trend upward together.
failure_condition: The joint upward trend of RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when the macro regime shifts to late-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate sustained economic growth and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth consistently outpaces quarterly GDP growth, it signals improving supply-side efficiency and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence between INDPRO and GDPC1 growth rates fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales coinciding with rising unemployment during late-cycle expansions signal weakening consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5%.
